name: alloc_iommu_preserves_all_pcid_mappings
code:
proof fn phi_4_alloc_iommu_preserves_all_pcid_mappings(
    old_mm: MemoryManager, new_mm: MemoryManager, pcid: Pcid, new_proc: ProcPtr, ret: IOid
)
    requires
        old_mm.wf(),
        old_mm.free_ioids.len() > 0,
        new_mm.wf(),
        new_mm.page_tables =~= old_mm.page_tables,
        new_mm.pcid_active(pcid) == old_mm.pcid_active(pcid),
        old_mm.pcid_active(pcid),
    ensures
        new_mm.get_pagetable_mapping_by_pcid(pcid) == old_mm.get_pagetable_mapping_by_pcid(pcid),
{
}

name: alloc_iommu_new_mapping_empty
code:
proof fn phi_5_alloc_iommu_new_mapping_empty(
    old_mm: MemoryManager, new_mm: MemoryManager, new_proc: ProcPtr, ret: IOid
)
    requires
        old_mm.wf(),
        old_mm.free_ioids.len() > 0,
        new_mm.wf(),
        new_mm.ioid_active(ret),
        !old_mm.ioid_active(ret),
        new_mm.iommu_tables =~= old_mm.iommu_tables,
    ensures
        new_mm.get_iommu_table_mapping_by_ioid(ret).dom() == Set::<PagePtr>::empty(),
{
}
reason: Newly activated IOMMU has empty mapping but the postcondition says iommu_tables are unchanged — the IOMMU was already present (just free), so its "empty" mapping comes from the free-implies-is_empty invariant rather than any initialization
